title: Calyptrosciadium bungei
taxonomy: Calyptrosciadium bungei is classified in the plant kingdom. It belongs to the family Apiaceae, which is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. Within this family, it is placed in the subfamily Apioideae. Further classification includes the tribe Apieae and the genus Calyptrosciadium. The species bungei is the only known member of this genus.

intro: Calyptrosciadium bungei, also known as Bunge's calyptrosciadium, is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Calyptrosciadium bungei include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is also used in various herbal formulations, including tinctures and capsules, to promote digestive health and boost the immune system.
